pass_summary: Core implementation is solid: health pid field, decideServeStartup decision helper with full test matrix, plugin version-aware ensureDashboard, dead notify removal, and README refresh all land correctly. Three defects stand out: (1) Task 4 left stale tool counts in both dev-rule files (26/28 instead of 29), causing Truth #6 to fail; (2) an ESRCH condition inversion in serve.ts produces a misleading log on the exact happy-path case (process already gone); (3) the _cachedBinaryVersion sentinel uses null for both 'not yet fetched' and 'binary not found', so a failed first lookup is never cached and re-spawns the binary on every call.

issues.title: Task 4 DoD unmet: tool counts not updated in dev-rule files
issues.description: `.sentinal/rules/sentinal-mcp-servers.md` line 3 still reads '28 tools across 6 domains' and the Memory section header on line 25 still reads '— 6 tools' (missing `memory_share`). `.sentinal/rules/sentinal-project.md` line 87 still reads '26 tools across 6 domains'. The actual count is 29 (confirmed via 29 `server.tool()` calls across src/). Task 4 DoD requires counts matching grep-verified registrations, and Truth #6 (`rg "29 tools" .sentinal/rules/sentinal-mcp-servers.md`) fails. This makes the dev rules actively misleading for anyone working on the MCP layer.
issues.suggested_fix: In `.sentinal/rules/sentinal-mcp-servers.md`: change '28 tools' → '29 tools' on line 3, change 'Memory Domain ... — 6 tools' → '— 7 tools' on line 25, add `memory_share` row to the Memory table. In `.sentinal/rules/sentinal-project.md`: change '26 tools' → '29 tools' on line 87.

issues.title: README MCP catalog totals still say 28 — Task 5 DoD partially unmet
issues.description: README.md line 59 reads 'sentinal (28 tools across 6 domains)' and line 566 reads 'exposes **28 tools across 6 domains**'. The catalog table itself lists 7 memory tools, 6 worktree tools, etc. (correct rows), but the two summary lines contradict it. The task DoD requires every count/flag to be grep-verified against source; 29 `server.tool()` registrations exist.
issues.suggested_fix: Update README.md line 59 and line 566: change '28 tools' → '29 tools'. Also update the Memory row count in the catalog table from '6' to '7' (add `memory_share` row), if not already listed.

issues.title: ESRCH condition is inverted in serve.ts takeover block — misleading log on normal path
getBinaryVersion null-caching ambiguity — failed lookups not cached, re-spawns binary on every call
Task 1 DoD: ESRCH kill case not unit-tested in lifecycle.test.ts
issues.description: Task 1 DoD requires '6+ cases incl. ESRCH' and the plan's decision matrix explicitly calls out 'kill throws ESRCH (process already gone → proceed to bind)'. The `lifecycle.test.ts` diff adds tests for: `start` (not running), `exit` (same version), `takeover` (older+pid), `takeover-no-pid` (older, no pid), `start` (no version), `start` (probe throws) — that is 6 cases, but none tests the ESRCH kill path. The ESRCH handling lives in `serve.ts` (not lifecycle.ts), and since CLI action handlers are not unit-testable directly, the plan intended to cover this via the injected `kill` fn. No such injected `kill` parameter exists on `decideServeStartup`, and no test exercises what happens when `process.kill` throws ESRCH.
issues.suggested_fix: Either: (a) add an optional `killFn` injectable to `decideServeStartup` or a new `performTakeover(opts)` helper exported from lifecycle.ts that accepts an injected `kill` fn, and add a test verifying ESRCH is swallowed; or (b) document that the ESRCH path is intentionally tested only via live smoke (acceptable if clearly marked in test comments).

issues.title: takeover-no-pid path in serve.ts structurally unreachable after takeover block falls through
issues.description: In serve.ts, the `takeover-no-pid` guard (lines 72-77) checks `decision.action === "takeover-no-pid"` using the *original* `decision` — but this block is only reached after the `takeover` block (lines 51-70) finishes. If the original decision was `takeover`, `decision.action` is `"takeover"`, so the `takeover-no-pid` check on line 72 can never be true for that execution path. The `takeover-no-pid` branch only fires when the *original* probe returned `takeover-no-pid`, which is correct. The logic works correctly, but the code would be clearer with `else if` chaining or early returns to make the mutual exclusion explicit.

issues.title: Background serve path bypasses idempotency check entirely
issues.description: In serve.ts lines 36-38, `--background` mode returns immediately without calling `decideServeStartup`. The plan scope says 'Make `sentinal serve` idempotent and self-replacing' and the OpenCode plugin's `defaultDashboardSpawn` always passes `--background`. This means the plugin's spawn path (the primary automated path) never benefits from the idempotency logic — it relies entirely on the plugin's own `ensureDashboard` probe. This is consistent with the plan's Pre-Mortem #2 (ensureDashboard as the gate) but creates a gap if `--background` is called directly without going through the plugin.
issues.suggested_fix: Consider running the health probe in `startBackground()` as well (a quick abort if same version is already running), or document explicitly in a comment that `--background` intentionally skips the probe (relying on the caller to gate).

truths.truth: rg "pid: process.pid" src/dashboard/routes/api.ts matches (health exposes pid)
truths.evidence: src/dashboard/routes/api.ts healthHandler returns `json({ status: "ok", version: ctx.version, pid: process.pid })`. server.test.ts adds a test asserting `typeof body.pid === "number"` and `body.pid > 0`.

truths.truth: curl returns JSON containing "pid:" (runtime truth — static only)
truths.evidence: Code change is confirmed correct (pid: process.pid added to health response). Runtime verification requires a live dashboard; not statically provable.

truths.truth: rg "api/health" and "--version" both match in sentinal.ts AND autoStartProcess("server.pid", "serve") does NOT match
truths.evidence: sentinal.ts: `defaultDashboardProbe` fetches `http://127.0.0.1:41778/api/health`; `getBinaryVersion` runs `execFileAsync(binPath, ["--version"])`. The old `autoStartProcess("server.pid", "serve")` call site replaced with `void ensureDashboardForTest(...)`. grep confirms no `autoStartProcess("server.pid", "serve")` call remains.

truths.truth: rg -l "sentinal notify" targets/ returns no files AND embedded-assets.ts contains zero matches
truths.evidence: grep confirms zero matches for 'sentinal notify' across all targets/ files (excluding dist/). embedded-assets.ts also returns no matches. All 4 files (spec-bugfix-plan.md, spec-verify.md, both SKILL.md equivalents) updated.

truths.truth: rg "sentinal update" README.md and rg "memory setup" README.md both match
truths.evidence: README.md contains multiple matches for 'sentinal update' (lines 84, 207, 211+) and 'sentinal memory setup' (lines 112, 165, 181, 217, 244). Both truths confirmed.

`.sentinal/rules/sentinal-mcp-servers.md` line 3 still reads '28 tools across 6 domains'. The Memory section (line 25) still reads '— 6 tools'. `.sentinal/rules/sentinal-project.md` line 87 reads '26 tools'. Actual grep count: 29 `server.tool()` registrations in src/. Task 4 is marked complete but the counts were not updated.

truths.truth: Running sentinal serve while same-version dashboard is live exits 0 without second process (live smoke)
truths.evidence: Code path confirmed: decideServeStartup returns 'exit' → logDashboard + process.exit(0). Unit tests cover the decision logic. Live smoke not verifiable statically; no CI evidence in diff.
